entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The legume crop affected by collar rot and associated with the tested antifungal bacteria

entities.name: COLLAR ROT
entities.description: A fungal disease of groundnut caused by Aspergillus niger

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS NIGER
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes collar rot in groundnut

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS FLAVUS
entities.description: A necrotrophic fungal pathogen of groundnut

entities.name: RHIZOCTONIA BATATICOLA
entities.description: A necrotrophic fungal pathogen of groundnut

entities.name: RHIZOCTONIA SOLANI
entities.description: A necrotrophic fungal pathogen of groundnut

entities.name: SCLEROTIUM ROLFSII
entities.description: A necrotrophic fungal pathogen of groundnut

entities.name: CERCOSPORA ARACHIDICOLA
entities.description: A biotrophic fungal pathogen of groundnut

entities.name: PHAEOISARIOPSIS PERSONATA
entities.description: A biotrophic fungal pathogen of groundnut

entities.name: PUCCINIA ARACHIDIS
entities.description: A biotrophic fungal pathogen of groundnut

entities.name: PSEUDOMONAS SP. GRS 175
entities.description: A bacterium showing broad-spectrum antifungal activity against groundnut pathogens

entities.name: PSEUDOMONAS AERUGINOSA GPS 21
entities.description: A bacterium showing broad-spectrum antifungal activity against groundnut pathogens

entities.name: PSEUDOMONAS AERUGINOSA GSE 18
entities.description: A bacterium that is an effective biocontrol agent for collar rot and is tolerant to thiram

entities.name: PSEUDOMONAS AERUGINOSA GSE 19
entities.description: A bacterium that is an effective biocontrol agent for collar rot

entities.name: PSEUDOMONAS AERUGINOSA GSE 30
entities.description: A bacterium showing broad-spectrum antifungal activity against groundnut pathogens

entities.name: NECROTROPHIC FUNGI
entities.description: Fungi that kill host cells to obtain nutrients, including A. flavus, A. niger, R. bataticola, R. solani, and S. rolfsii

entities.name: BIOTROPHIC FUNGI
entities.description: Fungi that derive nutrients from living host cells, including C. arachidicola, P. personata, and P. arachidis

entities.name: ANTIFUNGAL ACTIVITY
entities.description: The ability of bacteria to inhibit the growth or germination of fungi

entities.name: CELL-FREE CULTURE FILTRATES
entities.description: Bacterial extracts used to test for fungicidal activity and mycelial deformation

entities.name: MYCELIAL DEFORMATIONS
entities.description: Structural changes such as hyphal bulging and vacuolization in necrotrophic fungi

entities.name: SPORE GERMINATION
entities.description: The process of fungal spore growth, which can be inhibited by bacterial filtrates

entities.name: BIOCONTROL
entities.description: The use of biological agents to suppress the collar rot disease

entities.name: SEED TREATMENT
entities.description: The application of biocontrol agents to seeds to reduce rotting and wilting

entities.name: PRE-EMERGENCE ROTTING
entities.description: The rotting of seeds before they emerge from the soil

entities.name: POSTEMERGENCE WILTING
entities.description: The wilting of plants after they have emerged from the soil

entities.name: RHIZOSPHERE
entities.description: The soil region surrounding the groundnut roots colonized by P. aeruginosa GSE 18

entities.name: PEAT FORMULATION
entities.description: A formulation of P. aeruginosa GSE 18 used for biocontrol application

entities.name: THIRAM
entities.description: A fungicide to which P. aeruginosa GSE 18 exhibits tolerance

entities.name: TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of P. aeruginosa GSE 18 to survive in the presence of thiram

entities.name: FUNGICIDE
entities.description: Chemical agents used in combination with biocontrol agents for improved disease control

entities.name: INTEGRATED MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A strategy combining biocontrol agents and fungicides to manage collar rot

entities.name: GREENHOUSE
entities.description: The controlled environment where the effectiveness of biocontrol agents was tested
